rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Injunctive Relief, Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2024, Line 1, PETITIONER KLAUS BIESENBACH's Petition For Injunctive Relief, Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance. Denied. Petitioner's proof of service shows that respondents were not served with the required notice per Code of Civil Procedure 1005, even assuming, which is uncertain, that petitioners were properly served with the summons and petition permitting this court to exercise jurisdiction over them. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AMHERST ASSOCIATES CONSTRUCTION MANAGEMENT INC. VS. RICHARD G. BROWN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Amherst Associates Construction Management, Inc.'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2024, Line 3, DEFENDANT RICHARD BROWN's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Amherst Associates Construction Management, Inc.'s Complaint. Defendant Richard Brown's motion to dismiss is denied. Plaintiff has shown that Mr. Brown has sought to evade service of process and that it acted diligently which is a valid exception per CCP 583.240(d) to mandatory dismissal. Significantly, Mr. Brown does not deny that he has sought to evade service of process. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Leave To Proceed Anonymously And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2024, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE's Motion Seeking Leave To Proceed Anonymously. Plaintiff Jane Doe's motion for leave to proceed anonymously is granted. Plaintiff has shown that she has a legitimate and substantial privacy interest in proceeding anonymously and that her privacy interest outweighs the public's interest in knowing her name. Defendant Eric Rheinheimer has not shown that he would suffer legal prejudice by the granting of this motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: COLBY SUTTON MODLIN ET AL VS. LUCID GROUP USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2024, Line 9, DEFENDANT LUCID GROUP USA INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Granted. The court exercised its discretion to consider the untimely opposition papers. Assuming that the plaintiff asserts that the delegation clause is itself unconscionable, plaintiff fails to show any procedural unconscionability as to the delegation clause due to the opt out provision which he chose not to exercise. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)
